title: Fixed(I,F) arithmetic operators require undisclosed compiler changes or are wrong
description: The spec shows 'var z: Q16_16 = x + y' for Fixed types and claims 'Fixed is a standard library type, not a compiler primitive... No compiler changes required.' But Zig does not support operator overloading for custom types. The + operator on Fixed(16,16) would not call Fixed's addition method without compiler support. Either Fixed requires compiler recognition as an intrinsic (like bf16), or + doesn't work and callers must write x.add(y) — which the spec also shows in separate examples. The spec is inconsistent: the overview uses operator syntax, the detailed arithmetic section uses method syntax. Callers can't know which is correct.
